title: cynoglossum heterocarpum var. mariolense
intro: Cynoglossum heterocarpum var. mariolense, also known as the "Mary's tongue orchid" or "Mary's tongue toadflax", is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant that belongs to the borage family (Boraginaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Cynoglossum heterocarpum var. mariolense include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and flowers have been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions.
